details: The "Craftsmen Training Scheme" was launched by the Directorate General of Training, Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, Government of India. The scheme aims to ensure a steady flow of skilled workers in different trades for the domestic industry, to raise quantitatively and qualitatively the industrial production by systematic training, to reduce unemployment among the educated youth by providing them with employable skills, and to cultivate and nurture a technical and industrial attitude in the minds of the younger generation. The scheme provides systematic vocational training through a network of Industrial Training Institutes and National Skill Training Institutes located all over the country, offering comprehensive skill development programs in various trades and conducting All India Trade Tests for certification.
benefits: Varies from 6 months to 2 years, depending on the trade.
National Council for Vocational Training certificate upon successful completion.
Hands-on practical training in various industrial trades.
Enhanced employability in domestic and international markets.
Access to 15,042 Industrial Training Institutes and 33 National Skill Training Institutes.
Training on the latest industrial equipment and technology.
Training by qualified instructors and industry experts.
Conducted by the Directorate General of Training under the aegis of the National Council for Vocational Training at the end of training.
Certificates recognized across India for employment.
Training aligned with current industry requirements and standards.
Minimum 80% attendance is required to appear in the Final Trade Test.
Regular participation in practical training sessions and workshops.
Compliance with the institute rules and regulations.
Successful completion of periodic assessments and tests.
eligibility: The applicant must be an Indian citizen.
The applicant must meet the age requirements as specified for different trades (generally between 14 to 40 years).
The applicant must have completed the minimum educational qualifications as prescribed for specific trades (ranging from 8th standard to 12th standard).
The applicant must be able to read and write in the local language / Hindi / English.
The applicant must be physically and medically fit to undergo the specific trade training.
The applicant must not have any physical disabilities that would prevent them from performing the trade activities.
The applicant must meet specific requirements for certain trades (such as color vision for electrical trades).
The applicant must demonstrate basic aptitude for the chosen trade.

offline: The candidate must visit the Industrial Training Institute and obtain the prescribed application form.
The applicant should fill out the application form completely with accurate personal details, educational background, trade preference, and attach all required documents with proper attestation.

The candidate must pay the prescribed application and training fees as applicable and submit the completed application form along with all supporting documents to the authorised staff of the Institute, within the specified deadline.

*Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicabl
The institutes will prepare merit lists based on educational qualifications and other criteria, and selected candidates will be notified about their admission through official communication channels.

Selected candidates must report to the institute on the specified date for document verification, admission confirmation, and completion of remaining formalities, including medical examination if required.
After successful admission confirmation, candidates will begin their vocational training as per the institute's academic calendar and training schedule for their respective trades.

Application deadlines vary by institute and are typically announced before the commencement of each academic session (usually April-May for the August session and October-November for the February session).

documents_required: Age Proof Certificate (Birth Certificate/10th Standard Certificate)
Income Certificate (For Fee Concession If Applicable)
Medical Fitness Certificate (From Registered Medical Practitioner)
Domicile Certificate (For State Quota If Applicable)

faqs.question: What are the minimum educational qualifications I need to have completed before I can apply for training in different trades?
faqs.answer: You must have completed minimum educational qualifications as prescribed for specific trades, which typically range from 8th standard to 12th standard depending on the particular trade you wish to pursue.

faqs.question: Do I need to demonstrate any special abilities or skills for certain trades before I can get admitted to the training program?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must demonstrate basic aptitude for your chosen trade, and certain trades have specific requirements such as color vision testing for electrical trades.

faqs.question: What happens if I miss too many training sessions during my vocational training period and my attendance falls below the required threshold?
faqs.answer: You need to maintain minimum 80% attendance throughout your training to be eligible to appear in the Final Trade Test, so poor attendance will disqualify you from certification.

faqs.question: How long will I need to dedicate to complete the entire vocational training program from start to finish?
faqs.answer: Training duration varies from 6 months to 2 years depending on the specific trade you choose to pursue.

faqs.question: What language proficiency do I need to have to successfully participate in the training program and understand the course materials?
faqs.answer: You must be able to read and write in either the local language, Hindi, or English to effectively participate in the training program.

faqs.question: Are there any physical health conditions or disabilities that would prevent me from being accepted into the training program?
faqs.answer: You must be physically and medically fit to undergo the specific trade training and must not have any physical disabilities that would prevent you from performing the trade activities.

faqs.question: What is the age range within which I must fall to be eligible for admission to the vocational training program?
faqs.answer: You must meet the age requirements specified for different trades, which generally fall between 14 to 40 years of age.

faqs.question: What kind of certificate will I receive after successfully completing my vocational training and passing all required examinations?
faqs.answer: You will receive a National Council for Vocational Training certificate upon successful completion, which is recognized across India for employment purposes.

faqs.question: Do I need to be an Indian citizen to apply for this vocational training program, or can foreign nationals also participate?
faqs.answer: You must be an Indian citizen to be eligible for this training program and must provide valid proof of residence.

faqs.question: What type of medical documentation do I need to submit to prove that I am fit for the physical demands of my chosen trade?
faqs.answer: You must submit a Medical Fitness Certificate issued by a Registered Medical Practitioner to demonstrate your fitness for the specific trade training.

faqs.question: How can I obtain the official application form and where should I go to start the application process for the training program?
faqs.answer: You must visit the Industrial Training Institute where you wish to train and obtain the prescribed application form from there.

faqs.question: What types of identity and educational documents do I need to gather and submit along with my completed application form?
faqs.answer: You need to submit educational certificates, age proof certificate, Aadhaar card copy, passport size photographs, and if applicable, caste certificate and income certificate for fee concessions.

faqs.question: When do the application deadlines typically occur throughout the year and how much advance notice is usually given to prospective applicants?
faqs.answer: Application deadlines vary by institute and are typically announced before each academic session, usually during April-May for August session and October-November for February session.

faqs.question: What should I do immediately after submitting my application to ensure I have proper proof of my application submission?
faqs.answer: You must request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority containing essential details such as date, time of submission, and unique identification number if applicable.
